mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% in Sept 2010 indicates post-crisis labor slack
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with improving labor dynamics
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 367.67B in Sept 2010 reflects post-crisis consumer recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays subdued, signaling extended monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and CPIAUCSL at 218.37 in Sept 2010
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with low inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals weakening economic capacity and future earnings headwinds, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.21 in Aug 2010 reflects post-crisis industrial weakness
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 1.5% MoM, SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength; when both rise simultaneously, forward equity returns accelerate as construction and consumer spending drive GDP growth and corporate profitability.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 2% MoM AND RSAFS rises > 1% MoM in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 598K and RSAFS at 367.67B in Aug-Sept 2010
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 150K monthly additions, signaling robust labor demand that supports consumer income and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.2M in Sept 2010 reflects post-crisis employment recovery
failure_condition: When PAYEMS MoM change > 150K, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 62% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.025.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated unemployment (UNRATE) creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real returns and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors demand higher risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% and CPIAUCSL at 218.37 in Sept 2010
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with high unemployment and rising prices
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 48% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.015.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1% annualized rate, signaling economic stagnation that compresses earnings multiples and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset lower.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13194.9B in Q2 2010 reflects post-crisis recovery plateau
failure_condition: When GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1%, SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
